title: oxycoccus palustris subsp. hagerupii
intro: Oxycoccus palustris subsp. hagerupii, also known as bog bilberry, is a sub-species of the Oxycoccus palustris plant, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). It is a low-growing, shrub-like plant native to the Arctic and sub-Arctic regions, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high anthocyanin content, which is responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bog bilberry has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Oxycoccus palustris subsp. hagerupii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
